description: Vertical-specific negotiation and red-flag parameters. Swap this file (and job_spec.schema.json) to retarget the whole system at a different phone-priced market - the agents and eval scripts should not need code changes.

red_flag_rule.description: A quote is flagged as a potential red flag, not a win, when either condition holds.

session_comparison.priority: primary - use this whenever 3 or more quotes have been gathered for the same job spec
session_comparison.method: flag any quote whose total_estimate is 30% or more below the median total_estimate of all quotes gathered for this job

static_benchmark_fallback.priority: fallback - use only when fewer than 3 quotes exist for this job
static_benchmark_fallback.source: FMCSA consumer guidance + moveBuddha aggregated quotes for comparable in-state residential moves
static_benchmark_fallback.note: Ranges below are illustrative starting points for a ~45-mile, 2-bedroom, standard-service move (the Rock Hill -> Charlotte reference case). Replace with your own benchmark data source per market size and distance band before relying on this in production.

negotiation_levers: cite a specific competing binding quote and ask to match or beat it
ask for a price match without naming the competitor
push on deposit percentage and refundability
push on fuel surcharge and stairs/long-carry fees specifically, not just the total
ask for packing materials or an extra service thrown in at no charge instead of a price cut
ask whether today's number is honored for 48-72 hours to allow comparison shopping
